file.content:
name: Claude Code

on:
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]
  pull_request_review_comment:
    types: [created]
  issues:
    types: [opened, assigned]
  pull_request_review:
    types: [submitted]

jobs:
  claude:
    if: |
      (github.event_name == 'issue_comment' && contains(github.event.comment.body, '@claude')) ||
      (github.event_name == 'pull_request_review_comment' && contains(github.event.comment.body, '@claude')) ||
      (github.event_name == 'pull_request_review' && contains(github.event.review.body, '@claude')) ||
      (github.event_name == 'issues' && (contains(github.event.issue.body, '@claude') || contains(github.event.issue.title, '@claude')))
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      issues: write
      id-token: write
      actions: read # Required for Claude to read CI results on PRs
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Checkout PR branch (if PR comment)
        if: github.event.issue.pull_request
        env:
          PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.issue.number }}
        run: |
          echo "Fetching PR #$PR_NUMBER..."
          git fetch origin pull/$PR_NUMBER/head:pr-$PR_NUMBER
          git checkout pr-$PR_NUMBER
          echo "Successfully checked out PR branch"

      - name: Run Claude Code
        id: claude
        uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}

          # This is an optional setting that allows Claude to read CI results on PRs
          additional_permissions: |
            actions: read

          # Optional: Give a custom prompt to Claude. If this is not specified, Claude will perform the instructions specified in the comment that tagged it.
          # prompt: 'Update the pull request description to include a summary of changes.'

          # Optional: Add claude_args to customize behavior and configuration
          # See https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code-action/blob/main/docs/usage.md
          # or https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/cli-reference for available options
          # claude_args: '--allowed-tools Bash(gh pr:*)'

29	# 3. TypeScript Tests (runs if libraries/typescript/** changed)
30	#    ├─ typescript-lint: ESLint checks
31	#    ├─ typescript-build: Build all packages
32	#    ├─ typescript/mcp-use: Unit tests + agent integration tests (requires API keys)
33	#    ├─ typescript/inspector: Unit tests for inspector package
34	#    ├─ typescript/cli: Unit tests for CLI package
35	#    └─ typescript-changeset-check: PR validation (PRs only)
36	#
37	# 4. create-mcp-use-app Tests (runs if packages/create-mcp-use-app/** changed)
38	#    ├─ create-mcp-use-app-build: Build and pack the CLI
39	#    └─ create-app-tests: Matrix [3 OS × 3 PM × 3 templates × 2 flags]
40	#       - Tests project creation across Ubuntu/macOS/Windows
41	#       - Tests npm, yarn, pnpm package managers
42	#       - Tests starter, mcp-ui, mcp-apps templates
43	#       - Collapsed into one expandable job for clean UI
44	#

48	on:

50	    branches: [main, canary]
51	  pull_request:
52	    branches: [main, canary]
53	  workflow_call:
54	    inputs:
55	      ref:
56	        description: "Commit SHA or ref to test"
57	        required: true
58	        type: string
59	      python:
60	        description: "Run Python jobs"
61	        required: false
62	        type: boolean
63	        default: true
64	      typescript:
65	        description: "Run TypeScript jobs"
66	        required: false
67	        type: boolean
68	        default: true
69	      create_mcp_use_app:
70	        description: "Run create-mcp-use-app jobs"
71	        required: false
72	        type: boolean
73	        default: false

76	        required: false
77	        type: boolean
78	        default: false

80	jobs:
81	  detect-changes:
82	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
83	    outputs:
84	      python: ${{ steps.out.outputs.python }}
85	      typescript: ${{ steps.out.outputs.typescript }}
86	      create-mcp-use-app: ${{ steps.out.outputs.create-mcp-use-app }}
87	    steps:
88	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
89	        with:
90	          ref: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_call' && inputs.ref || github.sha }}
91	      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@v3
92	        id: filter
93	        if: github.event_name != 'workflow_call'
94	        with:
95	          filters: |
96	            python:
97	              - 'libraries/python/**'
98	            typescript:
99	              - 'libraries/typescript/**'
100	            create-mcp-use-app:
101	              - 'libraries/typescript/packages/create-mcp-use-app/**'
102	      - name: Compute outputs
103	        id: out
104	        uses: actions/github-script@v7
105	        env:
106	          CI_INPUT_PYTHON: ${{ inputs.python }}
107	          CI_INPUT_TYPESCRIPT: ${{ inputs.typescript }}
108	          CI_INPUT_CREATE: ${{ inputs.create_mcp_use_app }}
109	        with:
110	          script: |
111	            const fromWfCall = context.eventName === 'workflow_call';
112	            const py = fromWfCall ? (process.env.CI_INPUT_PYTHON === 'true') : ("${{ steps.filter.outputs.python }}" === 'true');
113	            const ts = fromWfCall ? (process.env.CI_INPUT_TYPESCRIPT === 'true') : ("${{ steps.filter.outputs.typescript }}" === 'true');
114	            const create = fromWfCall ? (process.env.CI_INPUT_CREATE === 'true') : ("${{ steps.filter.outputs.create-mcp-use-app }}" === 'true');
115	            core.setOutput('python', String(py));
116	            core.setOutput('typescript', String(ts));
117	            core.setOutput('create-mcp-use-app', String(create));

252	  python-transport-tests-report:
253	    needs: python-transport-tests
254	    if: always() && github.event_name == 'pull_request' && needs.python-transport-tests.result != 'skipped'
255	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
256	    permissions:
257	      pull-requests: write
258	    steps:
259	      - name: Download all test results
260	        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
261	        with:
262	          pattern: transport-test-results-*
263	          path: test-results
264	          merge-multiple: false
265	      - name: Generate PR comment
266	        uses: actions/github-script@v7
267	        with:
268	          script: |
269	            const fs = require('fs');
270	            const path = require('path');
271	            
272	            const transports = ['stdio', 'sse', 'streamable_http'];
273	            let tableRows = [];
274	            let allPassed = true;
275	            
276	            for (const transport of transports) {
277	              const xmlPath = path.join('test-results', `transport-test-results-${transport}`, `test-results-${transport}.xml`);
278	              let status = '⏭️ Skipped';
279	              let tests = '-';
280	              let passed = '-';
281	              let failed = '-';
282	              let errors = '-';
283	              let time = '-';
284	              
285	              if (fs.existsSync(xmlPath)) {
286	                const content = fs.readFileSync(xmlPath, 'utf8');
287	                
288	                // Parse basic stats from JUnit XML
289	                const testsMatch = content.match(/tests="(\d+)"/);
290	                const failuresMatch = content.match(/failures="(\d+)"/);
291	                const errorsMatch = content.match(/errors="(\d+)"/);
292	                const timeMatch = content.match(/time="([\d.]+)"/);
293	                
294	                if (testsMatch) {
295	                  const totalTests = parseInt(testsMatch[1]);
296	                  const failureCount = failuresMatch ? parseInt(failuresMatch[1]) : 0;
297	                  const errorCount = errorsMatch ? parseInt(errorsMatch[1]) : 0;
298	                  const passedCount = totalTests - failureCount - errorCount;
299	                  
300	                  tests = totalTests.toString();
301	                  passed = passedCount.toString();
302	                  failed = failureCount.toString();
303	                  errors = errorCount.toString();
304	                  time = timeMatch ? `${parseFloat(timeMatch[1]).toFixed(2)}s` : '-';
305	                  
306	                  if (failureCount === 0 && errorCount === 0) {
307	                    status = '✅ Passed';
308	                  } else {
309	                    status = '❌ Failed';
310	                    allPassed = false;
311	                  }
312	                }
313	              }
314	              
315	              tableRows.push(`| ${transport} | ${status} | ${tests} | ${passed} | ${failed} | ${errors} | ${time} |`);
316	            }
317	            
318	            const overallStatus = allPassed ? '✅ All transport tests passed!' : '❌ Some transport tests failed';
319	            
320	            const comment = `## Python Transport Tests Results
321	            
322	            ${overallStatus}
323	            
324	            | Transport | Status | Tests | Passed | Failed | Errors | Time |
325	            |-----------|--------|-------|--------|--------|--------|------|
326	            ${tableRows.join('\n')}
327	            `;
328	            
329	            // Find existing comment
330	            const { data: comments } = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
331	              owner: context.repo.owner,
332	              repo: context.repo.repo,
333	              issue_number: context.issue.number,
334	            });
335	            
336	            const botComment = comments.find(c => 
337	              c.user.type === 'Bot' && 
338	              c.body.includes('## Python Transport Tests Results')
339	            );
340	            
341	            if (botComment) {
342	              await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
343	                owner: context.repo.owner,
344	                repo: context.repo.repo,
345	                comment_id: botComment.id,
346	                body: comment,
347	              });
348	            } else {
349	              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
350	                owner: context.repo.owner,
351	                repo: context.repo.repo,
352	                issue_number: context.issue.number,
353	                body: comment,
354	              });
355	            }
